---
title: 'What is worship?'
date: '2011-10-29T05:10:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '6152071276367396464'
blogger_filename: '/2011/10/what-is-worship.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'study'
  - 'Prayer Time'
  - 'Daily Reading'
---

<b>My worship is my work which causes people to see the nature of God.&nbsp;</b><br /><br /><b><u>ENTER IN TO WORSHIP</u></b><br />Did you know that "Enter Into Worship" is not a phrase in the bible? Not in any translation that I could find. There are many references to entering God's house, God's Temple, the place of worship... We are even told to enter in order to worship. But there doesn't seem to be a biblical precident for entering INTO worship. I found this odd because I've heard and used the phrase SO many times since childhood growing up in churches.<br /><br /><b>Psalm 5:7&nbsp;NLT&nbsp;</b><br />Because of your unfailing love, I can<b>&nbsp;enter your house</b>; with deepest awe&nbsp;<b>I will worship at your Temple.</b><br /><b>Jeremiah 7:2 ESV</b><br />"Stand in the gate of the LORD's house, and proclaim there this word, and say, Hear the word of the LORD, all you men of Judah who&nbsp;<b>enter these gates to worship the LORD</b>.<br /><b>Ezekiel 46:2 ESV</b><br />The prince shall enter by the vestibule of the gate from outside, and shall take his stand by the post of the gate. The priests shall offer his burnt offering and his peace offerings, and&nbsp;<b>he shall worship at the threshold of the gate</b>. Then he shall go out, but the gate shall not be shut until evening.<br /><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/psalms/100-4.html">Psalm 100:4</a></b><br />4 Enter his<b>&nbsp;gates with thanksgiving</b>, and his&nbsp;<b>courts with praise</b>! Give thanks to him; bless his name!<br /><br /><b><u>WHAT IS PRAISE?</u></b><br />Praise is to speak well of someone/something. If I am given a gift by a person I will thank them. If it is an&nbsp;extravagant&nbsp;gift I may not only thank them but begin telling them what a great person they are because of this gift and I'll tell other people about what a person they are because of this gift. This is the essence of praise. I am praising them. So I am to enter the realms of God with "Thanksgiving" and "Speaking well of Him". This is the attitude with which I enter His presence.<br /><br /><b><u>WHAT IS WORSHIP?</u></b><br />Worship is mentioned over <a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/search/?q=worship&amp;c=&amp;t=nkjv&amp;ps=100&amp;s=Bibles">105 times in the NKJV</a> on a basic search. Worship, both in &nbsp;Greek and Hebrew, means to bow down, fall to the knees, kiss the hand, or in other ways pay respect and homage to a superior of some kind. There are, however, several words translated worship. Another word sometimes translated worship is also translated serve or work. Our work and service are part of our worship before God.<br />Thoughts from my friend <a href="http://aldeanpearson.blogspot.com/">Al</a>:<br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial, sans-serif; font-size: 13px;"><i>The thought was based on the scripture,&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/matthew/5-16.html">Matt 5:16 (KJV)&nbsp;</a></i></span><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial, sans-serif; font-size: 13px;"><i></i></span><br /><i><span style="line-height: 1.5em; text-indent: 0px;">Let your&nbsp;<a href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="133388599ec58261_26537x3" saprocessedanchor="true" style="color: #5797b0;"></a>light&nbsp;<a href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="133388599ec58261_26537x4" saprocessedanchor="true" style="color: #5797b0;"></a>so&nbsp;<a href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="133388599ec58261_26537x5" saprocessedanchor="true" style="color: #5797b0;"></a>shine before men, that they may see your good works<b>[Worship]</b>, and glorify<b>[See the Nature of]</b>&nbsp;your Father<b>&nbsp;[The King]</b>which is in heaven.</span></i><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial, sans-serif; font-size: 13px;"><i><span style="line-height: 19px;">Your purpose is your WORK and your WORK is your WORSHIP!</span></i></span><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial, sans-serif; font-size: 13px;"><i></i></span><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial, sans-serif; font-size: 13px;"><i></i></span><br /><br /><b>AVODAH - Worship/Work Also written as Abad.&nbsp;</b><br /><b><u><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avodah">http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avodah</a></u></b><br /><b><a href="http://www.avodah.net/">http://www.avodah.net/</a></b><br /><br />The Hebrew word for Worship is also the same base/root as Work.<br />See Also:&nbsp;<a href="http://ag.org/top/church_workers/wrshp_gen_avodah.cfm">http://ag.org/top/church_workers/wrshp_gen_avodah.cfm</a><br /><br /><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 12px;"></span><br /><h3 class="PassageTitle" style="display: inline; font-size: 18px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 20px; margin-top: 11px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">      <span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 12px;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/kjv/exodus/3-12.html" id="" style="color: #3380cc; text-decoration: underline;" title="Go to Exodus 3:12 (Standard View)">Exodus 3:12</a>&nbsp;KJV</span></h3><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 12px;"></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">12</span>&nbsp;And he&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=0559" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">said</a>&nbsp;, Certainly I will be with thee; and this shall be a&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=0226" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">token</a>&nbsp;unto thee,&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=03588" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">that</a>&nbsp;I have&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=07971" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">sent</a>&nbsp;thee: When thou hast brought&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=03318" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">forth</a>&nbsp;the&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=05971" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">people</a>&nbsp;out of&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=04714" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">Egypt,</a>&nbsp;ye shall&nbsp;<b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=05647" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">serve</a>&nbsp;</b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=0430" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">God</a>&nbsp;upon this&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=02022" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">mountain.</a></span></div><div><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus%203:12;&amp;version=NIV;">Exodus 3:12 NIV</a>&nbsp;</div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">12</span>&nbsp;And God said, "I will be with you.<a href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="1"></a>&nbsp;And this will be the sign<a href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="2"></a>&nbsp;to you that it is I who have sent you: When you have brought the people out of Egypt, you<a href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="a"></a>&nbsp;will <b>worship </b>God on this mountain.<a href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="3"></a>"</span></div><div><br /></div><div>So...That which we do is our Worship. The Nature of God is Glory. So through my work/worship people are able to see the nature of God, My King, who is in heaven.<br /><br /><b><u>Worship is the work which brings men to see the nature of God</u>.&nbsp;</b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b>Everything I do, everything I am, is a revelation of who God is. In my triumphs I see His Grace. In my defeats I see His Mercy. In nature I see his handiwork. In people I see his heart. If I can get in touch with this I begin to see the heart of Jesus and after Him Paul. Living every breath to see the Kingdom Of God manifested on this earth.&nbsp;</b></div><div><br />Darrell G. Wolfe</div><div><br /></div><div><b><u>VERSES TO STUDY:</u></b></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 18px; font-weight: bold;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/kjv/john/4-24.html" id="" style="color: #3380cc; text-decoration: underline;" title="Go to John 4:24 (Standard View)">John 4:24</a></span></div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">24</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=2316" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">God</a>&nbsp;is a&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=4151" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">Spirit:</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=2532" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">and</a>&nbsp;they that&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=4352" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">worship</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=846" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">him</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=1163" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">must</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=4352" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">worship</a>&nbsp;him&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=1722" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">in</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=4151" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">spirit</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=2532" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">and</a>&nbsp;in&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=225" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">truth.</a></span><br /><div class="greek" style="font-family: BSTGreek; font-size: 21px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=4151" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">pneu'ma</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{N-NSN}</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=3588" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">oJ</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{T-NSM}</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=2316" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">qeov?,</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{N-NSM}</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=2532" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">kai;</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{CONJ}</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=3588" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">tou;?</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{T-APM}</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=4352" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">proskunou'nta?</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{V-PAP-APM}</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=846" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">aujto;n</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{P-ASM}</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=1722" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">ejn</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{PREP}</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=4151" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">pneuvmati</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{N-DSN}</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=2532" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">kai;</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{CONJ}</span>&nbsp;ajlhqeiva/&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{N-DSF}</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=1163" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">dei'</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{V-PQI-3S}</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=g&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=4352" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">proskunei'n.</a>&nbsp;<span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;">{V-PAN}</span><br /><span class="key" style="font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px;"><br /></span></div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 17px; font-weight: bold;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/passage.aspx?q=psalms+95:6&amp;t=kjv">Psalms 95:6 KJV&nbsp;Interlinear.&nbsp;</a></span><br /><h4 class="ResultTitle" style="clear: left; font-size: 17px; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">               <span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; font-weight: normal; line-height: 20px;"><div class="versetext" style="font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 10px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">6</span>&nbsp;O&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=0935" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">come</a>&nbsp;, let us&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=07812" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">worship</a>&nbsp;and bow&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=03766" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">down</a>&nbsp;: let us&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=01288" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">kneel</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=06440" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">before</a>&nbsp;the&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=03068" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">LORD</a>&nbsp;our&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=06213" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">maker</a>&nbsp;.</div><div class="hebrew" style="font-family: BSTHebrew; font-size: 24px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px; text-align: right;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=03068" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">h'wh.y</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=06440" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">-yen.pil</a>&nbsp;h'k.r.bin&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=03766" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">h'['r.kin.w</a>&nbsp;h,w]x;T.vin .Wa{<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=0935" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">B</a>&nbsp;? .Wnef{[</div></span></h4><h4 class="ResultTitle" style="clear: left; font-size: 17px; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">               </h4><h4 class="ResultTitle" style="clear: left; font-size: 17px; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">               </h4><h4 class="ResultTitle" style="clear: left; font-size: 17px; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">                  <a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/psalms/132-7.html" style="color: #3380cc; text-decoration: underline;">Psalm 132:7 NKJV</a></h4><div class="ResultText" style="font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Let us go into His tabernacle; Let us&nbsp;<span class="Highlight" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 2px; padding-right: 2px; padding-top: 0px;">worship</span>&nbsp;at His footstool.</div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 12px;"></span><br /><h4 class="ResultTitle" style="clear: left; font-size: 17px; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">                 <a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/jeremiah/26-2.html" style="color: #3380cc; text-decoration: underline;">Jeremiah 26:2 NKJV</a></h4><div class="ResultText" style="font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">"Thus says the Lord: 'Stand in the court of the Lord's house, and speak to all the cities of Judah, which come to&nbsp;<span class="Highlight" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 2px; padding-right: 2px; padding-top: 0px;">worship</span>&nbsp;in the Lord's house, all the words that I command you to speak to them. Do not diminish a word.</div><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/matthew/passage.aspx?q=matthew+4:5-15"><b>Matthew 4:8-11</b></a><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><span class="versetext" id="mt4-8" style="display: inline; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Again, the devil took Him up on an exceedingly high mountain, and showed Him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext" id="mt4-9" style="display: inline; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">9</span>&nbsp;And he said to Him, "All these things I will give You if You will fall down and worship me."&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext highlightThenFade" id="mt4-10" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">10</span>&nbsp;Then Jesus said to him, "Away with you,&nbsp;<a class="highlightThenFade" href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="c" style="background-color: #;"></a>Satan! For it is written, 'You shall worship the Lord your God, and Him only you shall serve.' "&nbsp;<a class="highlightThenFade" href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="d" style="background-color: #;"></a></span><span class="versetext" id="mt4-11" style="display: inline; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">11</span>&nbsp;Then the devil left Him, and behold, angels came and ministered to Him.</span></span><br /><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/ephesians/passage.aspx?q=ephesians+2:5-15"><b>Ephesians 2:5-15</b></a><br /><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><span class="versetext" id="eph2-8" style="display: inline; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">8</span>&nbsp;For by grace you have been saved through faith, and that not of yourselves; it is the gift of God,&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext" id="eph2-9" style="display: inline; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">9</span>&nbsp;not of works, lest anyone should boast.&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext highlightThenFade" id="eph2-10" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">10</span>&nbsp;For we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand that we should walk in them.&nbsp;</span></span><br /><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/2-corinthians/passage.aspx?q=2-corinthians+9:10-14">2 Corinthians 9</a></b><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><span class="versetext" id="2co9-10" style="display: inline; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">10</span>&nbsp;Now may He who supplies seed to the sower, and bread for food, supply and multiply the seed you have sown and increase the fruits of your righteousness,&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext" id="2co9-11" style="display: inline; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">11</span>&nbsp;while you are enriched in everything for all liberality, which causes thanksgiving through us to God.&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext" id="2co9-12" style="display: inline; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">12</span>&nbsp;For the administration of this service not only supplies the needs of the saints, but also is abounding through many thanksgivings to God,&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext" id="2co9-13" style="display: inline; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">13</span>&nbsp;while, through the proof of this ministry, they glorify God for the obedience of your confession to the gospel of Christ, and for your liberal sharing with them and all men,&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext" id="2co9-14" style="display: inline; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">14</span>&nbsp;and by their prayer for you, who long for you because of the exceeding grace of God in you.</span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 17px; font-weight: bold;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/matthew/5-16.html" style="color: #3380cc; text-decoration: underline;">Matthew 5:16 NKJV</a></span><br /><div class="ResultText" style="font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works and&nbsp;<span class="Highlight" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 2px; padding-right: 2px; padding-top: 0px;">glorify</span>&nbsp;your Father in heaven.</div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 12px;"></span><br /><h3 class="PassageTitle" style="display: inline; font-size: 18px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 20px; margin-top: 11px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">   <a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/kjv/psalms/100-4.html" id="" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;" title="Go to Psalm 100:4 (Standard View)">Psalm 100:4</a></h3><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">4</span>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=0935" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">Enter</a>&nbsp;into his&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=08179" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">gates</a>&nbsp;with&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=08426" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">thanksgiving,</a>&nbsp;and into his&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=02691" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">courts</a>&nbsp;with&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=08416" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">praise:</a>&nbsp;be&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=03034" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">thankful</a>&nbsp;unto him, and&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=01288" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">bless</a>&nbsp;his&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=08034" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">name.</a></span></span><br /><div class="hebrew" style="font-family: BSTHebrew; font-size: 24px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px; text-align: right;">w{l -.Wdw{<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=0935" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">h</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=08416" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">h'Lih.tiB</a>&nbsp;wy't{<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=02691" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">rec]x</a>&nbsp;h'dw{<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=08426" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">t.B</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=08179" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">wy'r'[.v</a>&nbsp;.Wa{B ? w{m.v .Wk]r'B</div><br /><br /><b><u>DEFINITIONS:</u></b><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 14px;"><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/greek/kjv/proskuneo.html">The KJV New Testament Greek Lexicon "Worship":</a></b></span><br /><div style="margin-bottom: 14px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"></div><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="320"><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td nowrap="" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top">&nbsp;<b>Strong's Number:</b>&nbsp;&nbsp; 4352</td><td align="center" nowrap="" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="120">&nbsp;<b></b><b>&nbsp;</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top"><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Original Word</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Word Origin</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%"><span class="greek" style="font-family: BSTGreek; font-size: 21px;">proskunevw</span></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">from (<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/greek/kjv/pros.html" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">4314</a>) and a probable derivative of (<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/greek/kjv/kuon.html" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">2965</a>) (meaning to kiss, like a dog licking his master's hand)</td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Transliterated Word</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/greek/kjv/proskuneo.html#Legend" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">TDNT</a>&nbsp;Entry</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">Proskuneo</td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">6:758,948</td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Phonetic Spelling</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Parts of Speech</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%"><i>pros-koo-neh'-o</i>&nbsp;&nbsp;<span id="LexiconAudio" url="http://www.biblestudytools.com/resource/flash/player-button.swf?audio=http://ht.salemweb.net/bst/lexicons/greek-mp3/4352g.mp3"><object classid="clsid:D27CDB6E-AE6D-11cf-96B8-444553540000" height="17" type="application/x-shockwave-flash" width="17"><embed type="application/x-shockwave-flash" pluginspage="http://www.adobe.com/go/getflashplayer" src="http://www.biblestudytools.com/resource/flash/player-button.swf?audio=http://ht.salemweb.net/bst/lexicons/greek-mp3/4352g.mp3" width="17" height="17"></object></span>&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">Verb</td></tr></tbody></table></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">&nbsp;<b>Definition</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><br /><ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="1"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to kiss the hand to (towards) one, in token of reverence</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">among the Orientals, esp. the Persians, to<b> fall upon the knees and touch the ground with the forehead</b> as an expression of profound reverence</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">in the NT by<b> kneeling or prostration</b> to do homage (to one) or make obeisance, whether in order to express respect or to make supplication</li><ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="a"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">used of homage shown to men and beings of superior rank</li><ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="1"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to the Jewish high priests</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to God</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to Christ</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to heavenly beings</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to demons</li></ol></ol></ol></td></tr></tbody></table></td></tr></tbody></table><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 14px;"><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/hebrew/kjv/abad.html">The KJV Old Testament Hebrew Lexicon "Serve/Work/Worship"</a></b></span><br /><div style="margin-bottom: 14px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"></div><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="320"><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td nowrap="" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top">&nbsp;<b>Strong's Number:</b>&nbsp;&nbsp; 05647</td><td align="center" nowrap="" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="120">&nbsp;<b>&nbsp;</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top"><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Original Word</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Word Origin</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">db[</td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">a primitive root</td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Transliterated Word</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/hebrew/kjv/abad.html#Legend" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">TDNT</a>&nbsp;Entry</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">`abad</td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">TWOT - 1553</td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Phonetic Spelling</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Parts of Speech</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%"><i>aw-bad'</i>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">Verb</td></tr></tbody></table></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">&nbsp;<b>Definition</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="1"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to work, serve<ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="a"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">(Qal)<ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="1"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to labour, work, do work</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to work for another, serve another by labour</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to serve as subjects</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to serve (God)</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to serve (with Levitical service)</li></ol></li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">(Niphal)<ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="1"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to be worked, be tilled (of land)</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to make oneself a servant</li></ol></li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">(Pual) to be worked</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">(Hiphil)<ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="1"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to compel to labour or work, cause to labour, cause to serve</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to cause to serve as subjects</li></ol></li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">(Hophal) to be led or enticed to serve</li></ol></li></ol></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">&nbsp;<b>King James Word Usage - Total:</b>&nbsp;290</td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td align="top" colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">serve&nbsp;227, do&nbsp;15, till&nbsp;9, servant&nbsp;5, work&nbsp;5, worshippers&nbsp;5, service&nbsp;4, dress&nbsp;2, labour&nbsp;2, ear&nbsp;2, miscellaneous&nbsp;14</td></tr></tbody></table></td></tr></tbody></table><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 14px;"><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/hebrew/kjv/shachah.html">The KJV Old Testament Hebrew Lexicon "Worship"</a></b></span><br /><div style="margin-bottom: 14px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"></div><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="320"><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td nowrap="" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top">&nbsp;<b>Strong's Number:</b>&nbsp;&nbsp; 07812</td><td align="center" nowrap="" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="120">&nbsp;<b>&nbsp;</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top"><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Original Word</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Word Origin</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">hxX</td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">a primitive root</td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Transliterated Word</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/hebrew/kjv/shachah.html#Legend" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">TDNT</a>&nbsp;Entry</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">Shachah</td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">TWOT - 2360</td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Phonetic Spelling</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Parts of Speech</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%"><i>shaw-khaw'</i>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">Verb</td></tr></tbody></table></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">&nbsp;<b>Definition</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="1"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to bow down<ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="a"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">(Qal) to bow down</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">(Hiphil) to depress (fig)</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">(Hithpael)<ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="1"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">to bow down, prostrate oneself 1c</li></ol></li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">before superior in homage 1c</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">before God in worship 1c</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">before false gods 1c</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">before angel</li></ol></li></ol></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">&nbsp;<b>King James Word Usage - Total:</b>&nbsp;172</td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td align="top" colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">worship&nbsp;99, bow&nbsp;31, bow down&nbsp;18, obeisance&nbsp;9, reverence&nbsp;5, fall down&nbsp;3, themselves&nbsp;2, stoop&nbsp;1, crouch&nbsp;1, miscellaneous&nbsp;3</td></tr></tbody></table></td></tr></tbody></table><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 14px;"><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/hebrew/kjv/tehillah.html">The KJV Old Testament Hebrew Lexicon "Praise" Psalms 100:4</a></b></span><br /><div style="margin-bottom: 14px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"></div><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="320"><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td nowrap="" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top">&nbsp;<b>Strong's Number:</b>&nbsp;&nbsp; 08416</td><td align="center" nowrap="" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="120">&nbsp;<b></b><b>&nbsp;</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top"><table style="-webkit-border-horizontal-spacing: 0px; -webkit-border-vertical-spacing: 0px; border-collapse: collapse;"><tbody><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Original Word</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Word Origin</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%"><span class="hebrew" style="font-family: BSTHebrew; font-size: 24px;">hllht</span></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">from (<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/hebrew/kjv/halal.html" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">01984</a>)</td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Transliterated Word</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/hebrew/kjv/tehillah.html#Legend" style="color: #336699; text-decoration: none;">TDNT</a>&nbsp;Entry</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">T@hillah</td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">TWOT - 500c</td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Phonetic Spelling</b></td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" width="50%"><b>Parts of Speech</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%"><i>teh-hil-law'</i>&nbsp;&nbsp;<span id="LexiconAudio" url="http://www.biblestudytools.com/resource/flash/player-button.swf?audio=http://ht.salemweb.net/bst/lexicons/hebrew-mp3/8416h.mp3"><object classid="clsid:D27CDB6E-AE6D-11cf-96B8-444553540000" height="17" type="application/x-shockwave-flash" width="17"><embed type="application/x-shockwave-flash" pluginspage="http://www.adobe.com/go/getflashplayer" src="http://www.biblestudytools.com/resource/flash/player-button.swf?audio=http://ht.salemweb.net/bst/lexicons/hebrew-mp3/8416h.mp3" width="17" height="17"></object></span>&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" valign="top" width="50%">Noun Feminine</td></tr></tbody></table></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">&nbsp;<b>Definition</b></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><br /><ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="1"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">praise, song or hymn of praise<ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="a"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">praise, adoration, thanksgiving (paid to God)</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">act of general or public praise</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">praise-song (as title)</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">praise (demanded by qualities or deeds or attributes of God)</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">renown, fame, glory<ol style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;" type="1"><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">of Damascus, God</li><li style="list-style-image: initial; list-style-position: initial; list-style-type: none; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">object of praise, possessor of renown (fig)</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"></td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">&nbsp;<b>King James Word Usage - Total:</b>&nbsp;57</td></tr><tr style="height: 20px;"><td align="top" colspan="2" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">praise&nbsp;57</td></tr></tbody></table></td></tr></tbody></table><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 32px; font-weight: bold;"><a href="http://1828.mshaffer.com/d/word/praise" style="color: #7b8b4c; text-decoration: none;" title="[42179] 6 ">praise</a></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span"></span><br /><div style="color: #465131;"><b style="color: #dad496; font-weight: bold;">PRAISE</b>, n. s as z. [L. pretium.]</div><div style="color: #465131;"></div><br /><br /><br /><dd>1. Commendation bestowed on a person for his personal virtues or worthy actions, on meritorious actions themselves, or on any thing valuable; approbation expressed in words or song. Praise may be expressed by an individual, and in this circumstance differs from fame, renown, and celebrity, which are the expression of the approbation of numbers, or public commendation. When praise is applied to the expression of public approbation, it may be synonymous with renown, or nearly so. A man may deserve the praise of an individual, or of a nation.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd></dd><dd>There are men who always confound the praise of goodness with the practice.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd>2. The expression of gratitude for personal favors conferred; a glorifying or extolling.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd></dd><dd>He hath put a new song into my mouth, even praise to our God. Ps.40.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd>3. The object, ground or reason of praise.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd></dd><dd>He is thy praise,and he is thy God. Deut.10.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"><b style="color: #dad496; font-weight: bold;">PRAISE</b>, v.t. [L. tollo, extollo; pretium.]</div><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd>1. To commend; to applaud; to express approbation of personal worth or actions.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd></dd><dd>We praise not Hector, though his name we know<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd></dd><dd>Is great in arms; 'tis hard to praise a foe.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd>2. To extol in words or song; to magnify; to glorify on account of perfections or excellent works.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd></dd><dd>Praise him, all his angels, praise ye him, all his hosts. Ps.148.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd>3. To express gratitude for personal favors. Ps.138.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd>4. To do honor to; to display the excellence of.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd></dd><dd>All thy works shall praise thee, O Lord. Ps.145.</dd><br /><b><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;"></span></b><br /><br /><b><b>AVODAH - Worship/Work</b></b><br /><b><b><u><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avodah">http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avodah</a></u></b></b><br /><b><b><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hebrew_labor">http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hebrew_labor</a></b></b><br /><b><b><a href="http://www.avodah.net/">http://www.avodah.net/</a></b></b><br /><br /><b>The Hebrew word for Worship is also the same base/root as Work.</b><br /><b>See Also:&nbsp;<a href="http://ag.org/top/church_workers/wrshp_gen_avodah.cfm">http://ag.org/top/church_workers/wrshp_gen_avodah.cfm</a></b><br /><br /><b><u><a href="http://www.myjewishlearning.com/practices/Ethics/Business_Ethics/Themes_and_Theology/Value_of_Work/Work_as_Calling.shtml">http://www.myjewishlearning.com/practices/Ethics/Business_Ethics/Themes_and_Theology/Value_of_Work/Work_as_Calling.shtml</a></u></b><br /><b><a href="http://www.thehighcalling.org/attitude/do-you-feel-broken-and-fragmented">http://www.thehighcalling.org/attitude/do-you-feel-broken-and-fragmented</a></b><br /><br /><b><u>FURTHER STUDY:</u></b><br /><b>Other interesting articles on this subject that bear further reading:</b><br /><br />The Hebrew word for Worship is also the same base/root as Work.<br />See Also:&nbsp;<a href="http://ag.org/top/church_workers/wrshp_gen_avodah.cfm">http://ag.org/top/church_workers/wrshp_gen_avodah.cfm</a><br /><br /><a href="http://www.victorious.org/praise.htm">http://www.victorious.org/praise.htm</a><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Arial; font-size: 13px;">Have you ever noticed when “gifts of the Spirit” operate in a church service? The power and anointing of the Holy Spirit usually becomes evident, subsequent to a time of worship and praise. Some think that worship is a response after the Holy Spirit moves upon them. However, it’s the other way around. God’s presence responds when we move upon Him with worship! Lifting up Jesus Christ through praise and worship invokes the Lord’s presence and power to flow in our midst.</span><br /><br /><a href="http://www.myjewishlearning.com/practices/Ethics/Business_Ethics/Themes_and_Theology/Value_of_Work/Work_as_Calling.shtml">http://www.myjewishlearning.com/practices/Ethics/Business_Ethics/Themes_and_Theology/Value_of_Work/Work_as_Calling.shtml</a><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial, verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 18px;">The word for work in Hebrew,&nbsp;<i style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">avodah</i>, is the same used for prayer. Avodah connotes service. (It is also the word for slavery, which is involuntary service.) Work is not only a necessary part of life, it is a form of service to the world, to the rest of humanity, and to God. We are meant to be of service, to be partners with God in the ongoing creation of the world. Yet even as we serve God, we also serve our fellow human beings, as set forth in this story about the Hasidic master Rabbi David of Lelov [Poland, 1746-1813]:</span><br /><br /><a href="http://www.homegrownpraise.com/WhatWorship.htm">http://www.homegrownpraise.com/WhatWorship.htm</a><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px;">Some have made the mistake of equating praise with "the outer court" and worship with "the inner court." To place this strong a barrier between the two is artificial.&nbsp;</span><br /><br /><a href="http://www.bibleabookoftruth.com/WhyChristiansPraiseAndWorshipGod.pdf">http://www.bibleabookoftruth.com/WhyChristiansPraiseAndWorshipGod.pdf</a><br /><br /><a href="http://www.khouse.org/articles/2005/557/">http://www.khouse.org/articles/2005/557/</a><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 17px;"></span><br />True worship, then, is contingent upon our offering ourselves as a living sacrifice and God cleansing our flesh and spirit. In other words, our heart condition matters more in worship than our voice, our good intentions or our actions. Without a cleansed heart, we simply cannot enter His presence or worship.<br />It's interesting to note that today worship music sales have absolutely skyrocketed, but statistics show us that there are more problems now in the Christian body than ever before. Why? If people are buying worship music, why aren't our lives reflecting this?<br />The answer is simple: we are becoming like what we worship! (<a href="http://www.blueletterbible.org/kjv/Psa/Psa135.html#18" style="color: #0c4481; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Psalm 135:18</a>)<br />We are praising God, but not truly worshiping Him. We are singing unto Him, making melody in our heart, but not allowing Him to cleanse our hearts and truly change us into His image. Much of it goes back to the fact that many Christians don't really know what it means to love God. They don't know how to daily surrender and relinquish themselves to Him. Without this knowledge, not only will there be no changed lives, there also will be no worshipers. Worship comes from love. We worship that which we love. In other words, the more we love Him, the more we will be able to worship Him.<br /><br />The following was SO good I had to re post it here for this study!<br /><a href="http://ag.org/top/church_workers/wrshp_gen_avodah.cfm">http://ag.org/top/church_workers/wrshp_gen_avodah.cfm</a><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 18px;"></span><br /><h1 style="clear: left; color: #666666; font-family: Arial, Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 22px;">       Word Study: (<i>AVODAH</i>) — Work/Worship</h1><div style="color: #666666; font: normal normal normal 12px/18px Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">BY WILLIAM D. BJORAKER</div><div style="color: #666666; font: normal normal normal 12px/18px Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;"><i>Avodah</i><b>&nbsp;</b>is the transliteration of the Hebrew word for&nbsp;<i>worship</i>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<i>work</i>. Have you ever thought about the connection? Is worship work?
